fieldRegistry.label: Primary Reason For Home Health / Medical Necessity

fieldGroups.label: Medical Necessity And Homebound
fieldGroups.description: Narratives and evidence for home health need and homebound status.

fieldGroups.label: Diagnosis And Coding
fieldGroups.description: Diagnosis evidence, coding-sensitive rows, and escalation flags.

fieldGroups.label: Medications, Allergies, And Special Treatments
fieldGroups.description: Medication safety, allergies, injectables, and O0110 special treatments.

fieldGroups.label: Functional And Therapy Status
fieldGroups.description: GG/M functional scoring, therapy need, discipline frequency, and goals.

fieldGroups.label: Symptom And Body System Review
fieldGroups.description: Body-system review sections that QA checks for consistency and completeness.

fieldGroups.label: Risk And Safety Assessments
fieldGroups.description: Fall, wound, hospitalization, and safety-related risk tools.

fieldGroups.label: Care Plan And Coordination
fieldGroups.description: Plan-of-care orders, patient summary, skilled interventions, and care plan content.

fieldGroups.label: Supplementals And History
fieldGroups.description: Past medical history, caregiver support, living situation, immunizations, and supplementals.

fieldGroups.label: Low Priority Admin Reference
fieldGroups.description: Identity, agency, physician, and demographic reference values that should not dominate QA.

HOME HEALTH REFERRAL ORDERS, F2F EVIDENCE SUMMARY, AND CERTIFICATION OF ELIGIBILITY

Encompass Health Rehabilitation Hospital of Henderson

intensive rehabilitation therapy as described above in an inpatient hospital environment
this patient can reasonably be expected to participate in and benefit from an inpatient

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS: This is an 82-year-old female with history of stroke who
reportedly had acute emesis and diarrhea.
felt that she had declined and recommended acute rehab,
PAST MEDICAL HISTORY: Significant for previous CVA with residual
SOCIAL HEALTH HISTORY: Nonsmoker, nonalcoholic drinker.
MEDICATIONS: Aspirin 81 mg daily, atorvastatin 10 mg daily, Lovenox 40 mg subcutaneously
daily, gabapentin 600 mg daily, Keppra 250 mg daily, levothyroxine 88 mcg daily,
pantoprazole 40 mg daily, and sertraline 50 mg daily.
REVIEW OF SYSTEMS: A 12-point system review was performed, otherwise negative except
NECK: Negative JVD or carotid bruits.
NEUROMUSCULAR EXAMINATION: Cranial nerves II-XII are grossly intact with no focal
Manual muscle testing of the upper extremities are 5/5 and lower extremity
should be in acute rehab unit rather than a lower level of setting of care.
she will need multidisciplinary approach, three hours a day of therapy for five days a
week, close medical supervision for 24 hours, and 24-hour rehab nursing care.
information described in a preadmission screening document.
implementation of an intensive rehabilitation program (3 hours per day, 5 days a week)
or substantially influenced the plan of care
due to complexity of nursing, medical management and rehabilitation needs.
stay with an interdisciplinary team approach to the delivery of rehabilitation care under
the direction and supervision of a rehabilitation physician.
Impairment of activities of daily living: Continue
Check laboratory data on a regular basis.
goals are to be established following completion of the assessment period.
also with activities of daily living.
